events.label: reasoning
events.text: We need answer JSON only. Need assess scenario: field service, HVAC-R, brazing & evacuation. Observed: vacuum not held at target microns (decay test - read gauge, FD). Work rough-complete. References: mfr specs, ACCA, EPA 608. Need decide pass/fail/needs_more_info. Since vacuum decay test failed (not held at target microns), evacuation non-compliant. Brazing maybe not directly observed but scope includes brazing & evacuation. Need determine if work meets applicable code/spec/manufacturer. Vacuum not held indicates leak or moisture/contamination, non-compliant. Decision fail. Risk? If refrigerant system not evacuated properly, can cause system damage, inefficiency, environmental release, maybe high? In service call, vacuum not held means leak; if charged could leak refrigerant. Risk high? Could be medium/high. Let's think. Non-compliant evacuation: high risk due to potential refrigerant leaks, moisture/acid, compressor damage. But not immediate life safety. Use high. Work_stage rough-complete. Component conditions: installed-defective (vacuum not held), non-compliant. percent_complete maybe 70. value numeric reading? Not given specific microns. Could set 0 because no numeric value provided? The schema says use value for numeric reading or computed quantity when task asks for one. Not asked. Could leave 0. confidence 0.95. event_time 0, rate 0. order empty. workable empty. findings: vacuum decay test failed; system will not hold target vacuum; potential leak or contamination. actions: leak test system, repair leaks, replace filter-drier, re-evacuate to manufacturer specified microns, verify decay rate, document. rationale: EPA 608 and mfr specs require evacuation to specified microns and holding vacuum; failure to hold indicates non-compliance. references: EPA 608 (venting prohibition, evacuation requirements), ACCA, mfr specs. Need ensure JSON valid. No prose outside.

events.label: response
events.text:
{
  "decision": "fail",
  "risk": "high",
  "work_stage": "rough-complete",
  "component_conditions": ["installed-defective", "non-compliant"],
  "percent_complete": 70,
  "value": 0,
  "sound_source": "",
  "confidence": 0.95,
  "event_time": 0.0,
  "rate": 0.0,
  "order": [],
  "workable": [],
  "findings": [
    "Vacuum decay test failed: system did not hold target microns.",
    "Evacuation is incomplete/non-compliant, indicating a likely leak, moisture, or non-condensable contamination.",
    "Refrigerant circuit integrity and OEM evacuation requirements are not satisfied."
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Perform a standing leak test (pressurized with dry nitrogen or approved tracer) to locate and repair leaks.",
    "Replace the liquid-line/filter-drier if contaminated or opened.",
    "Re-evacuate the system to the manufacturer-specified micron level.",
    "Repeat the vacuum decay test and verify the gauge holds at target microns for the required duration.",
    "Document final micron reading and decay rate before charging and start-up."
  ],
  "rationale": "EPA 608 prohibits venting and mandates proper evacuation, while OEM specs and ACCA require the system to reach and hold a specified vacuum. A vacuum that does not hold at target microns fails the decay test, so the brazing/evacuation work is non-compliant and cannot be closed out.",
  "references": [
    "EPA 608 - refrigerant venting prohibition and evacuation requirements",
    "ACCA standards - proper evacuation and dehydration procedures",
    "OEM manufacturer specifications - target microns and vacuum decay acceptance"
  ]
}
